analysis_population.exclusion_rule: reuse the frozen Record160 excluded_no_successful_source rule exactly; no post-hoc exclusion is allowed

bootstrap.statistic: equal mean of per-query parsed records per authoritative successful request
bootstrap.stream_derivation: base seed XOR first 64 bits of SHA-256(source NUL successful_request_yield)

failure_taxonomy.partial_failure: at least one authoritative successful request and at least one failed request for the same query and source
failure_taxonomy.redaction: only normalized category, stable hashes, counts, and enumerated status are emitted; raw error text is never emitted

rules.http_failure: audited error/status metadata contains an HTTP status or explicit 4xx/5xx marker
rules.parse_failure: audited error/status metadata contains schema, parse, decode, malformed, validation, JSON, XML, or payload-shape evidence
rules.provider_failure: audited error/status metadata identifies provider/source failure without transport, HTTP, or parse evidence
rules.snapshot_missing: a frozen logical call declares a Snapshot key that is absent or unreadable
rules.transport_failure: audited error/status metadata contains timeout, DNS, TLS, socket, connection, or network evidence
rules.unknown_failure: a failed Snapshot or terminal has no stronger auditable category

funnel.counting_unit: unified paper identity within query and source unless the field explicitly names requests or parsed records
funnel.loss_rate: known loss divided by the immediately preceding known stage; null when either side is unknown

stage_evidence.authoritative_snapshot: Snapshot key and validated retrieval Snapshot entry
stage_evidence.budget_retained_identity: identity remains after the existing global 200-candidate source-coverage budget
stage_evidence.canonical_identity: production unified identity profile built from a parsed Paper
stage_evidence.constraint_survivor: identity has a non-irrelevant production current_rules judgement in initial_judged
stage_evidence.global_unique_identity: identity appears after production cross-source unified identity deduplication and before the global candidate budget
stage_evidence.parsed_record: validated Paper records stored in the retrieval Snapshot response
stage_evidence.raw_provider_record: only counted when a frozen artifact explicitly records pre-parser provider record count; otherwise unknown
stage_evidence.source_unique_identity: production unified identity deduplication over successful parsed records attributed to that source
stage_evidence.top20_identity: identity appears in frozen final_returned and retains the source in its merged provenance

query_structure.boolean_operator: case-insensitive standalone AND, OR, or NOT
query_structure.quote_characters: ASCII single/double quotes and Unicode curly single/double quotes
query_structure.reporting: for each predeclared stratum and source report query count, failed query count/rate, legal-empty query count/rate, and unknown count
query_structure.unicode_class_precedence: ascii_only when all code points are ASCII; otherwise non_ascii_letter when any non-ASCII Unicode letter exists; otherwise non_ascii_nonletter

reconstruction.source_path: reuse source_fusion_ablation_v1 production canonicalization, unified identity deduplication, source-coverage truncation, judgement, reranking, and selection

request_accounting.duplicate_reference_rule: repeat references to the same key are reported but the response is consumed once for funnel counts
request_accounting.duplicate_violation: duplicate consumption is a violation only when it changes parsed, canonical, unique, or Top-20 counts
request_accounting.no_key_terminal: preserve frozen not_started/timeout/failure evidence without inventing a Snapshot response
request_accounting.shared_key_across_queries: account independently per opaque query; never merge populations

silent_loss_invariants: parsed records cannot exceed an explicitly known raw provider count
canonical identities cannot exceed parsed records
source unique identities cannot exceed canonical identities
global unique, budget-retained, and Top-20 source identities must trace to a canonical source identity
a failed-only query/source cannot expose parsed or Top-20 records
a legal-empty success cannot expose parsed records
unknown schema or parser evidence cannot be silently classified as legal empty
duplicate Snapshot references cannot multiply response records

state_classification.unknown_rule: when frozen evidence cannot distinguish adjacent stages or a failure category, emit unknown and preserve all known counts

statistical_unit.component_effect: query-equal mean within each frozen connected component
statistical_unit.estimand: equal mean over frozen connected components
statistical_unit.membership: reuse component_id from the frozen query assignment file without recomputation

warnings: Availability, record yield, identity coverage, and Top-20 contribution do not establish relevance, Precision, Recall, or an official score.
Frozen failures remain in the fixed 160-query population and are never used for post-hoc filtering.
Missing pre-parser evidence is reported as unknown and never reconstructed from parsed papers.
